Title: Lead CLAUDE.md with python -m harness universal form - fix Windows+Git-Bash PATH gotcha caught by 2nd fresh session
Notes: Operator feedback 2026-05-25 (iteration 3 on fresh-session orientation): pasted the bootstrap+orient one-liner from prior W13-FRESH-CLONE-BOOTSTRAP commit. Result: pip install -e . succeeded but agent reported 'harness.exe isn't on PATH in this shell - falling back to module form per the CLAUDE.md guidance'. Root cause: Windows + Git Bash classic - pip install creates harness.exe in Python's Scripts dir (e.g. C:/Users/<user>/AppData/Local/Microsoft/WindowsApps/Python313/Scripts/), but Git Bash doesn't have that dir on PATH. So bare 'harness' still returns command not found EVEN AFTER successful pip install. The agent self-recovered correctly to PYTHONPATH=src form per CLAUDE.md's fallback note, but the prompt should have led with the universal form. THE FIX: python -m harness <verb> is the universal post-install form - works whenever import harness works, no PATH dependency at all. SHIPPED: (1) CLAUDE.md: bootstrap one-liner now uses 'pip install -e . --quiet && python -m harness today && python -m harness plan show' (NOT bare 'harness today'). Section explains the Windows+Git-Bash gotcha explicitly + when bare 'harness' works. PYTHONPATH=src form demoted from primary fallback to last-resort. (2) tests/test_claudemd_invocation_works.py: 2 new tests (test_claudemd_recommends_python_m_harness_form proximity-checks 'python -m harness today' within first 80 lines; test_claudemd_recommended_prompt_has_python_m_form asserts the recommended-prompt section uses python -m harness, not bare harness). 7 tests now total. (3) tests/test_install_verify.py: 2 new slow-marked end-to-end tests (test_python_m_harness_works_post_install runs python -m harness today/plan/capabilities --help against the freshly-installed venv; test_orientation_one_liner_works_post_install actually runs python -m harness today + plan show end-to-end with cwd=repo to prove the recommended one-liner works). FINAL recommended minimal session-resume prompt (works on Windows + Git Bash + Linux + Mac + restricted shells): 'Resume xaxiu-harness. Run pip install -e . --quiet && python -m harness today && python -m harness plan show. Propose next action.' 7/7 invocation tests pass.

Title: Cross-provider survey of which subscription tiers enforce client allowlists
Notes: Operator question 2026-05-25: "Each AI providers have their allow list if you are on their subscription package, can you list those that don't, or have lax rules." RESEARCHED across 4 web searches covering Kimi/MiMo/GLM/Qwen/DeepSeek/Doubao/Anthropic/OpenRouter/Together/Fireworks/Groq/Cerebras. KEY PATTERN DISCOVERED — clean economic rule that predicts every observed case: "If you pay a flat-rate subscription you get a client allowlist. If you pay per-token you do not." Anchor-priced subscription tiers (0-100/mo for 00+/mo equivalent API usage) must gate misuse via UA/OAuth allowlists; pay-per-token APIs price misuse out directly so they do not need gating. STRICT ALLOWLIST (do not spoof, termination risk): Moonshot Kimi Code Token Plan (CONFIRMED TERMINATED our account), Anthropic Claude Pro/Max OAuth (banned third-party tools Feb 2026, reinstated with credit-pool catch June 2026), Zhipu GLM Coding Plan sk-sp- keys (TOS prohibits automated scripts/custom backends), Alibaba Qwen Coding Plan (TOS: intended only for Claude Code / Qwen Code; not curl/Postman/Dify). ADVISORY/LAX (TOS has allowlist but enforcement is currently permissive): Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan (xaxiu-harness/1.0 works TODAY but could tighten any time — matches Kimi pre-termination posture). OPEN (no client allowlist, safe by design — all pay-per-token APIs): DeepSeek API, Kimi Platform PAYG (api.moonshot.ai), Anthropic API key path (not OAuth), OpenAI API, Gemini API, MiMo PAYG (sk- keys), Doubao, Zhipu GLM PAYG (standard endpoint). AGGREGATORS (most lax — designed for arbitrary clients): OpenRouter (org-level model/provider allowlists only, no UA gate), Together AI, Fireworks, Groq, Cerebras Inference, Hyperbolic, Hugging Face Inference, Replicate. APPLIED TO HARNESS POOL: (1) DeepSeek = keep as primary (PAYG, open). (2) Kimi tp- = drop per W14-KIMI-REPLACEMENT-WITH-GLM. (3) MiMo tp- = keep with caveat; consider sk- PAYG insurance key. (4) GLM-5.1 (master plan candidate) = use PAYG endpoint open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4, AVOID the Coding Plan sk-sp- subscription endpoint (same termination class as Kimi). (5) Anthropic / Gemini = safe to add via API key, NEVER via OAuth. (6) Qwen Coding Plan = DO NOT use; use Qwen PAYG via DashScope or aggregator. (7) New row recommended: add OpenRouter or Together AI as outage-insurance aggregator (one config gateway to many models, zero termination risk). THREE PRACTICAL INSURANCE MOVES the harness should adopt: (a) Default UA stays xaxiu-harness/1.0 forever (already shipped W14-MIMO-TOS-COMPLIANCE). Truthful identification = never caught lying. (b) Prefer PAYG keys over subscription keys; warn operator when key prefix indicates subscription (sk-sp-, tp-). (c) Add an aggregator engine for outage insurance.
